title: crash_info.c:18 crash_info_store lacks enum bounds check
description: Contract violation: crash_info_store() - The 'reason' parameter is enum crash_reason but no bounds check ensures it's a valid enum value. If caller passes an out-of-range value (e.g., from cast or memory corruption), the stored value will be garbage. WHY this is a bug: Crash info is for post-mortem debugging; invalid reason codes make debugging harder. HOW to fix: Add 'if (reason >= CRASH_UNKNOWN) reason = CRASH_UNKNOWN;' at the start, or use CRASH_UNKNOWN as the default upper bound.
